title: Rollup.js Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for Rollup.js commands, syntax, and configuration.

commands.description: Initialize a new Rollup configuration file
commands.scenario: Initialize a new Rollup configuration file

commands.description: Watch files for changes and rebuild automatically
commands.scenario: Watch files for changes and rebuild automatically

commands.command: export default { input: 'src/index.js', output: { file: 'dist/bundle.js', format: 'cjs' } };
commands.description: Basic configuration for input and output
commands.scenario: Basic configuration for input and output

commands.command:
import { terser } from 'rollup-plugin-terser';
export default { input: 'src/index.js', plugins: [terser()] };
commands.description: Add a plugin to the configuration
commands.scenario: Add a plugin to the configuration

commands.command: export default { input: 'src/index.js', output: [{ file: 'dist/bundle.cjs.js', format: 'cjs' }, { file: 'dist/bundle.esm.js', format: 'esm' }] };

import babel from '@rollup/plugin-babel';
export default { input: 'src/index.js', plugins: [babel({ babelHelpers: 'bundled' })] };
export default { input: 'src/index.js', external: ['lodash'], output: { file: 'dist/bundle.js', format: 'cjs' } };
export default { input: 'src/index.js', output: { dir: 'dist', format: 'esm' }, preserveModules: true };

commands.description: Generate a bundle with a specific environment variable
commands.scenario: Generate a bundle with a specific environment variable

commands.description: Analyze the bundle size and contents
commands.scenario: Analyze the bundle size and contents

commands.command: Ensure all necessary plugins (e.g., Babel) are configured correctly.
Check module imports for circular references and refactor code.
Verify plugin installation and compatibility with Rollup version.
